lines: +summary: Track Painless allocation on derived and inherited allowlist methods
+ * Copyright Elasticsearch B.V. and/or licensed to Elasticsearch B.V. under one
+ * or more contributor license agreements. Licensed under the "Elastic License
+ * 2.0", the "GNU Affero General Public License v3.0 only", and the "Server Side
+ * Public License v 1"; you may not use this file except in compliance with, at
+ * your election, the "Elastic License 2.0", the "GNU Affero General Public
+ * License v3.0 only", or the "Server Side Public License, v 1".
+ * Marks an allowlisted method/constructor that allocates, charged against the per-context limit before the call. The size is
+ * always produced by an estimator — a {@code public static long} method taking the target's Java signature (receiver
+ * first for instance methods) — named by {@code class} and {@code method} and resolved at allowlist load time through the
+ * annotated class's class loader (so plugins may ship their own). A fixed cost is just an estimator that ignores its arguments
+ * @param estimatorClassName estimator's fully-qualified binary class name
- * Parses {@code @allocates_dynamic[class="fully.qualified.Class", method="methodName"]}. Only the shape is validated here;
- * resolution happens at allowlist load time so a missing estimator fails loudly.
+ * Parses {@code @allocates[class="fully.qualified.Class", method="estimate"]}. Only the shape is validated here; the estimator
+ * is resolved at allowlist load time so a missing one fails loudly.
-    public static final AllocatesDynamicAnnotationParser INSTANCE = new AllocatesDynamicAnnotationParser();
+    public static final AllocatesAnnotationParser INSTANCE = new AllocatesAnnotationParser();
-                "[@" + AllocatesDynamicAnnotation.NAME + "] requires [" + CLASS + "] and [" + METHOD + "] arguments"
+                "[@" + AllocatesAnnotation.NAME + "] requires [" + CLASS + "] and [" + METHOD + "] arguments"
-            throw new IllegalArgumentException("[@" + AllocatesDynamicAnnotation.NAME + "] [" + CLASS + "] must not be empty");
+            throw new IllegalArgumentException("[@" + AllocatesAnnotation.NAME + "] [" + CLASS + "] must not be empty");
-            throw new IllegalArgumentException("[@" + AllocatesDynamicAnnotation.NAME + "] [" + METHOD + "] must not be empty");
+            throw new IllegalArgumentException("[@" + AllocatesAnnotation.NAME + "] [" + METHOD + "] must not be empty");
- * Copyright Elasticsearch B.V. and/or licensed to Elasticsearch B.V. under one
- * or more contributor license agreements. Licensed under the "Elastic License
- * 2.0", the "GNU Affero General Public License v3.0 only", and the "Server Side
- * Public License v 1"; you may not use this file except in compliance with, at
- * your election, the "Elastic License 2.0", the "GNU Affero General Public
- * License v3.0 only", or the "Server Side Public License, v 1".
- * Marks an allowlisted constructor or method that allocates a fixed number of {@code bytes}, charged against the per-context
- * allocation limit before the call executes. The declared cost is the total heap allocation attributable to the call,
- * including transitive JDK-internal allocations. Use {@link AllocatesDynamicAnnotation} when the size is argument-dependent.
- * {@code 0} is a valid no-op ("audited: does not allocate") and emits no pre-check; negatives are rejected at allowlist load time.
- * Copyright Elasticsearch B.V. and/or licensed to Elasticsearch B.V. under one
- * or more contributor license agreements. Licensed under the "Elastic License
- * 2.0", the "GNU Affero General Public License v3.0 only", and the "Server Side
- * Public License v 1"; you may not use this file except in compliance with, at
- * your election, the "Elastic License 2.0", the "GNU Affero General Public
- * License v3.0 only", or the "Server Side Public License, v 1".
- * Parses {@code @allocates_constant[bytes="40b"]}: a single {@code bytes} argument holding a non-negative
- * {@link ByteSizeValue} (a unit is required except for {@code "0"}).
-    public static final AllocatesConstantAnnotationParser INSTANCE = new AllocatesConstantAnnotationParser();
-            throw new IllegalArgumentException("[@" + AllocatesConstantAnnotation.NAME + "] requires a single [" + BYTES + "] argument");
-                "[@" + AllocatesConstantAnnotation.NAME + "] [" + BYTES + "] argument must be a byte size value [" + value + "]",
-                "[@" + AllocatesConstantAnnotation.NAME + "] [" + BYTES + "] argument must not be negative [" + value + "]"
- * Copyright Elasticsearch B.V. and/or licensed to Elasticsearch B.V. under one
- * or more contributor license agreements. Licensed under the "Elastic License
- * 2.0", the "GNU Affero General Public License v3.0 only", and the "Server Side
- * Public License v 1"; you may not use this file except in compliance with, at
- * your election, the "Elastic License 2.0", the "GNU Affero General Public
- * License v3.0 only", or the "Server Side Public License, v 1".
- * Marks an allowlisted constructor or method whose allocation size depends on its arguments. An estimator — a
- * {@code public static long} method taking the target's full Java signature (receiver first for instance methods) — is invoked
- * at runtime and its result charged against the per-context allocation limit before the call executes. The estimator is named
- * by its {@code class} (fully-qualified binary name; JVM {@code $} form for inner classes) and {@code method}, resolved at
- * allowlist load time through the annotated class's class loader so plugins may ship their own.
- * @param estimatorClassName fully-qualified binary class name declaring the estimator
- * @param estimatorMethodName name of the estimator method
-/** Unit tests for the {@code @allocates_constant} and {@code @allocates_dynamic} allowlist annotation parsers. */
+/** Unit tests for the {@code @allocates[class=…, method=…]} allowlist annotation parser. */
+    private static final AllocatesAnnotationParser ALLOCATES = AllocatesAnnotationParser.INSTANCE;
+    private static AllocatesAnnotation parse(Map<String, String> arguments) {
-        // @allocates_constant[bytes="0"] is a valid no-op ("audited: does not allocate").
-        // ByteSizeValue requires a unit for anything but "0"; a bare number must not parse.
- * Built-in {@code @allocates_dynamic} estimators: {@code public static long} methods matching the annotated target's full Java
+ * Built-in {@code @allocates} estimators: {@code public static long} methods matching the annotated target's full Java
* signature (receiver first for instance methods), returning the total heap cost of the call. Invoked from generated bytecode
* on the pre-check path, so they must be cheap, must not allocate, and must not throw — even on argument values the
* annotated call itself would reject. Results are normalized by {@link AllocationGuard#sanitizeEstimate(long)}.
@@ -331,8 +340,217 @@ public static long toArrayBytes(Collection<?> receiver, Object[] array) {
+    // ---- Fixed-cost estimators. These allocate a constant-sized object independent of their arguments, so each just returns the
+    // ---- measured empty-instance (or thin-wrapper) heap cost. Signatures still match the annotated target so the pre-check path
+    // ---- can invoke them like any other estimator.
+    /** {@code new java.lang.StringBuffer()}: empty buffer shell plus its default 16-char backing array. */
+    /** {@code new java.lang.StringBuilder()}: empty builder shell plus its default 16-char backing array. */
+    public static long geoPointBytes(double lat, double lon) {
+    /** {@code new java.util.ArrayDeque()}: shell plus the default 16-element backing array. */
+    /** {@code new java.util.ArrayList()}: shell only; the backing array is created lazily on first add. */
+    /** {@code new java.util.BitSet()}: shell plus the default single-word backing array. */
+    /** {@code new java.util.HashMap()}: shell only; the table is created lazily on first put. */
+    /** {@code new java.util.HashSet()}: set shell plus the backing {@link java.util.HashMap} shell. */
+    /** {@code new java.util.Hashtable()}: shell plus its default 11-slot table. */
+    /** {@code new java.util.IdentityHashMap()}: shell plus its large default table. */
+    /** {@code new java.util.LinkedHashMap()}: {@link java.util.HashMap} shell plus the ordering header. */
+    /** {@code new java.util.LinkedHashSet()}: set shell plus the backing {@link java.util.LinkedHashMap} shell. */
+    /** {@code new java.util.LinkedList()}: empty list shell (nodes are allocated per element on add). */
+    /** {@code new java.util.PriorityQueue()}: shell plus the default 11-element backing array. */
+    /** {@code new java.util.Stack()}: {@link java.util.Vector} shell plus its default 10-element backing array. */
+    /** {@code new java.util.TreeMap()}: shell only (nodes are allocated per entry on put). */
+    /** {@code new java.util.TreeSet()}: set shell plus the backing {@link java.util.TreeMap} shell. */
+    /** {@code new java.util.Vector()}: shell plus its default 10-element backing array. */
+    /** {@code ScriptTermStats} accessors that each build a fresh fixed-field {@code StatsSummary}. */
+    /** {@code BigDecimal.valueOf(double)}: bounded-precision result from a double. */
+    /** {@code BigInteger.valueOf(long)}: magnitude fits in a single small {@code int[]}. */
+    /** {@code String.sha1()} augmentation: a fixed 40-char hex digest. */
+    /** {@code String.sha256()} augmentation: a fixed 64-char hex digest. */
+    /** {@code String.sha512()} augmentation: a fixed 128-char hex digest. */
+    /** {@code Arrays.asList(array)}: a fixed-size view wrapping the given array (the array itself is not copied). */
+    /** {@code Collections.nCopies(n, element)}: a fixed-size immutable list view backed by the single element. */
+    public static long collectionsNCopiesBytes(int n, Object element) {
+    /** {@code Collections.singletonMap(key, value)}: a fixed single-entry immutable map. */
+    public static long collectionsSingletonMapBytes(Object key, Object value) {
+    /** {@code Collections.singleton(o)}/{@code singletonList(o)}: a fixed single-element immutable view. */
+    /** {@code Collections.newSetFromMap(map)}/{@code unmodifiableMap(map)}: a thin fixed-size wrapper. */
-     * Heap cost of an {@code ArrayList} instance excluding its backing array; matches the {@code @allocates_constant} value on
+     * Heap cost of an {@code ArrayList} instance excluding its backing array; matches the {@code @allocates} value on
* the no-arg {@code new ArrayList()}, whose backing array is lazily created.
-     * Clamps an {@code @allocates_dynamic} estimator's result to {@code [0, Long.MAX_VALUE / 2]} before it is charged: a
+     * Clamps an {@code @allocates} estimator's result to {@code [0, Long.MAX_VALUE / 2]} before it is charged: a
* negative result (an estimator bug) must not credit the running total, and a huge one must trip any configurable limit
* without overflowing it (so estimators may return {@code Long.MAX_VALUE} for "definitely over"). Estimators must not
* throw; a thrown exception propagates and fails the script.
